name: CAPITAL PROPERTIES INC /RI/
business: The Company operates in one segment consisting principally of the leasing of Company-owned land in Capital Center and adjacent property in downtown Providence, Rhode Island under long-term ground leases, and also leases billboard locations to Lamar Outdoor Advertising, LLC. The Company owns approximately 18 acres in Capital Center and controls 23 outdoor advertising locations.

exposures.sentence: The Company operates in one segment that consists principally of the leasing of Company-owned land in Capital Center ( Capital Center ) and property adjacent to Capital Center (Parcel 20) in downtown Providence, Rhode Island under long-term ground leases with terms of 99 years or more.

The Company, through its wholly-owned subsidiary Tri-State Displays, Inc. leases 23 outdoor advertising locations containing 44 billboard faces along interstate and primary highways in Rhode Island and Massachusetts to Lamar Outdoor Advertising, LLC ( Lamar ) under a lease which expires in 2057.

The COVID-19 pandemic had an adverse impact on Metropark s parking operations as the move by many companies to a hybrid workplace model (one that mixes in-office and remote work) resulted in lower demand for parking spaces.

catalysts.event: Entered into long-term ground lease of Parcel 20, tenant possession conditional on approvals
catalysts.sentence: On January 25, 2024, the Company entered into a long-term ground lease of Parcel 20.

catalysts.event: Second Amendment with Metropark effective, fixing monthly rent at $57,000
catalysts.sentence: In January 2024, the Company entered into a Second Amendment to its Lease Agreement whereby Metropark agreed to return to a fixed monthly rental payment of $57,000 per month effective January 1, 2024.

catalysts.event: Superior Court entered judgment for Sprague for $173,000; paid on January 29, 2025
catalysts.sentence: On January 24, 2025 the Superior Court entered judgment for Sprague in the amount of $173,000 which includes, as required by law, prejudgment interest at 12% per annum. The $173,000 judgment was paid on January 29, 2025.

catalysts.event: Expected expenditure of $114,000 for environmental remediation in 2025
catalysts.sentence: At December 31, 2024, the Company s accrual for the remaining cost of remediation was $343,000 of which $114,000 is expected to be expended in 2025.
